creator: Alexander Terp
description:
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-14 (rad v0.10.1): still reproduces.

Calling zip() with fill=null truncates to the shortest list - same as no fill. But fill=0, fill=false, fill="" all correctly pad to the longest.

**Repro:**
a = [1, 2, 3]
b = [4, 5]
print(zip(a, b, fill=null))
// Actual:   [[1, 4], [2, 5]]            (truncated, same as no fill)
// Expected: [[1, 4], [2, 5], [3, null]]

**Root cause:** `core/funcs.go:~966` (was cited as :950) - the fill default is null and the code checks IsNull() to decide truncate-vs-pad, so explicit fill=null is indistinguishable from "not provided."

**Design/impl:** track whether the fill arg was explicitly passed rather than using null as the sentinel.
title: zip(fill=null) behaves same as no fill argument
